NKJV.1: A Song. A Psalm of David. O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and give praise, even with my glory.
NKJV.2: Awake, lute and harp! I will awaken the dawn.
NKJV.3: I will praise You, O LORD, among the peoples, And I will sing praises to You among the nations.
NKJV.4: For Your mercy is great above the heavens, And Your truth reaches to the clouds.
NKJV.5: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens, And Your glory above all the earth;
NKJV.6: That Your beloved may be delivered, Save with Your right hand, and hear me.
NKJV.7: God has spoken in His holiness: “I will rejoice; I will divide Shechem And measure out the Valley of Succoth.
NKJV.8: Gilead is Mine; Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim also is the helmet for My head; Judah is My lawgiver.
NKJV.9: Moab is My washpot; Over Edom I will cast My shoe; Over Philistia I will triumph.”
NKJV.10: Who will bring me into the strong city? Who will lead me to Edom?
NKJV.11: Is it not You, O God, who cast us off? And You, O God, who did not go out with our armies?
NKJV.12: Give us help from trouble, For the help of man is useless.
NKJV.13: Through God we will do valiantly, For it is He who shall tread down our enemies.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> or Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. O God<S>430</S>, my heart<S>3820</S> is fixed<S>3559</S>; I will sing<S>7891</S> and give praise<S>2167</S>, even with my glory<S>3519</S>.
KJV.2: Awake<S>5782</S>, psaltery<S>5035</S> and harp<S>3658</S>: I myself will awake<S>5782</S> early<S>7837</S>.
KJV.3: I will praise<S>3034</S> thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, among the people<S>5971</S>: and I will sing praises<S>2167</S> unto thee among the nations<S>3816</S>.
KJV.4: For thy mercy<S>2617</S> is great<S>1419</S> above the heavens<S>8064</S>: and thy truth<S>571</S> reacheth unto the clouds<S>7834</S>. <sup>clouds: or, skies</sup>
KJV.5: Be thou exalted<S>7311</S>, O God<S>430</S>, above the heavens<S>8064</S>: and thy glory<S>3519</S> above all the earth<S>776</S>;
KJV.6: That thy beloved<S>3039</S> may be delivered<S>2502</S>: save<S>3467</S> with thy right hand<S>3225</S>, and answer<S>6030</S> me.
KJV.7: God<S>430</S> hath spoken<S>1696</S> in his holiness<S>6944</S>; I will rejoice<S>5937</S>, I will divide<S>2505</S> Shechem<S>7927</S>, and mete out<S>4058</S> the valley<S>6010</S> of Succoth<S>5523</S>.
KJV.8: Gilead<S>1568</S> is mine; Manasseh<S>4519</S> is mine; Ephraim<S>669</S> also is the strength<S>4581</S> of mine head<S>7218</S>; Judah<S>3063</S> is my lawgiver<S>2710</S>;
KJV.9: Moab<S>4124</S> is my washpot<S>7366</S> <S>5518</S>; over Edom<S>123</S> will I cast out<S>7993</S> my shoe<S>5275</S>; over Philistia<S>6429</S> will I triumph<S>7321</S>.
KJV.10: Who will bring<S>2986</S> me into the strong<S>4013</S> city<S>5892</S>? who will lead<S>5148</S> me into Edom<S>123</S>?
KJV.11: Wilt not thou, O God<S>430</S>, who hast cast us off<S>2186</S>? and wilt not thou, O God<S>430</S>, go forth<S>3318</S> with our hosts<S>6635</S>?
KJV.12: Give<S>3051</S> us help<S>5833</S> from trouble<S>6862</S>: for vain<S>7723</S> is the help<S>8668</S> of man<S>120</S>.
KJV.13: Through God<S>430</S> we shall do<S>6213</S> valiantly<S>2428</S>: for he it is that shall tread down<S>947</S> our enemies<S>6862</S>.

ESV.1: My heart is steadfast, O God! I will sing and make melody with all my being!
ESV.2: Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awake the dawn!
ESV.3: I will give thanks to you, O LORD, among the peoples; I will sing praises to you among the nations.
ESV.4: For your steadfast love is great above the heavens; your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
ESV.5: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens! Let your glory be over all the earth!
ESV.6: That your beloved ones may be delivered, give salvation by your right hand and answer me!
ESV.7: God has promised in his holiness:  “With exultation I will divide up Shechem and portion out the Valley of Succoth.
ESV.8: Gilead is mine; Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet, Judah my scepter.
ESV.9: Moab is my washbasin; upon Edom I cast my shoe; over Philistia I shout in triumph.”
ESV.10: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
ESV.11: Have you not rejected us, O God? You do not go out, O God, with our armies.
ESV.12: Oh grant us help against the foe, for vain is the salvation of man!
ESV.13: With God we shall do valiantly; it is he who will tread down our foes.

NASB.1: A Song, a Psalm of David. My heart is steadfast, O God; I will sing, I will sing praises, even with my soul.
NASB.2: Awake, harp and lyre; I will awaken the dawn!
NASB.3: I will give thanks to You, O LORD, among the peoples, And I will sing praises to You among the nations.
NASB.4: For Your lovingkindness is great above the heavens, And Your truth [reaches] to the skies.
NASB.5: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens, And Your glory above all the earth.
NASB.6: That Your beloved may be delivered, Save with Your right hand, and answer me!
NASB.7: God has spoken in His holiness: 'I will exult, I will portion out Shechem And measure out the valley of Succoth.
NASB.8: 'Gilead is Mine, Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim also is the helmet of My head; Judah is My scepter.
NASB.9: 'Moab is My washbowl; Over Edom I shall throw My shoe; Over Philistia I will shout aloud.'
NASB.10: Who will bring me into the besieged city? Who will lead me to Edom?
NASB.11: Have not You Yourself, O God, rejected us? And will You not go forth with our armies, O God?
NASB.12: Oh give us help against the adversary, For deliverance by man is in vain.
NASB.13: Through God we will do valiantly, And it is He who shall tread down our adversaries.

NLT.1:
My heart is confident in you, O God;
no wonder I can sing your praises with all my heart!
NLT.2:
Wake up, lyre and harp!
I will wake the dawn with my song.
NLT.3:
I will thank you, LORD, among all the people.
I will sing your praises among the nations.
NLT.4:
For your unfailing love is higher than the heavens.
Your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
NLT.5:
Be exalted, O God, above the highest heavens.
May your glory shine over all the earth.
NLT.6:
Now rescue your beloved people.
Answer and save us by your power.
NLT.7:
God has promised this by his holiness :
“I will divide up Shechem with joy.
I will measure out the valley of Succoth.
NLT.8:
Gilead is mine,
and Manasseh, too.
Ephraim, my helmet, will produce my warriors,
and Judah, my scepter, will produce my kings.
NLT.9:
But Moab, my washbasin, will become my servant,
and I will wipe my feet on Edom
and shout in triumph over Philistia.”
NLT.10:
Who will bring me into the fortified city?
Who will bring me victory over Edom?
NLT.11:
Have you rejected us, O God?
Will you no longer march with our armies?
NLT.12:
Oh, please help us against our enemies,
for all human help is useless.
NLT.13:
With God’s help we will do mighty things,
for he will trample down our foes.

WEB.1: A Song. A Psalm by David. My heart is steadfast, God. I will sing and I will make music with my soul.
WEB.2: Wake up, harp and lyre! I will wake up the dawn.
WEB.3: I will give thanks to you, Yahweh, among the nations. I will sing praises to you among the peoples.
WEB.4: For your loving kindness is great above the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
WEB.5: Be exalted, God, above the heavens! Let your glory be over all the earth.
WEB.6: That your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand, and answer us.
WEB.7: God has spoken from his sanctuary: “In triumph, I will divide Shechem, and measure out the valley of Succoth.
WEB.8: Gilead is mine. Manasseh is mine. Ephraim also is my helmet. Judah is my scepter.
WEB.9: Moab is my wash pot. I will toss my sandal on Edom. I will shout over Philistia.”
WEB.10: Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who has led me to Edom?
WEB.11: Haven’t you rejected us, God? You don’t go out, God, with our armies.
WEB.12: Give us help against the enemy, for the help of man is vain.
WEB.13: Through God, we will do valiantly. For it is he who will tread down our enemies.

AMP.1:
A Song. A Psalm of David. O God, my heart is steadfast [with confident faith];
I will sing, I will sing praises, even with my soul.
AMP.2:
Awake, harp and lyre;
I will awaken the dawn!
AMP.3:
I will praise and give thanks to You, O LORD, among the people;
And I will sing praises to You among the nations.
AMP.4:
For Your lovingkindness is great and higher than the heavens;
Your truth reaches to the skies.
AMP.5:
Be exalted [in majesty], O God, above the heavens,
And Your glory above all the earth.
AMP.6:
That Your beloved [ones] may be rescued,
Save with Your right hand, and answer me!
AMP.7:
God has spoken in His holiness:
"I will rejoice, I will portion out Shechem [as I divide Canaan among My people],
And measure out the Valley of Succoth.
AMP.8:
"Gilead is Mine, Manasseh is Mine;
Ephraim also is the helmet of My head [My stronghold, My defense];
Judah is My scepter.
AMP.9:
"Moab is My washbowl;
Over Edom I will throw My shoe [to show Edom is Mine];
Over Philistia I will shout [in triumph]."
AMP.10:
Who will bring me into the fortified city [of Petra]?
Who will lead me to Edom?
AMP.11:
Have You not rejected us, O God?
And will You not go out, O God, with our armies?
AMP.12:
Give us help against the adversary,
For deliverance by man is in vain [a worthless hope].
AMP.13:
With God we will do valiantly,
For it is He who will trample down our enemies.

MSG.1:
A David prayer
I'm ready, God, so ready,
ready from head to toe.
Ready to sing,
ready to raise a God-song:
MSG.2:
"Wake, soul! Wake, lute!
Wake up, you sleepyhead sun!"
MSG.3:
I'm thanking you, God, out in the streets,
singing your praises in town and country.
MSG.4:
The deeper your love, the higher it goes;
every cloud's a flag to your faithfulness.
MSG.5:
Soar high in the skies, O God!
Cover the whole earth with your glory!
MSG.6:
And for the sake of the one you love so much,
reach down and help me—answer me!
MSG.7:
That's when God spoke in holy splendor:
"Brimming over with joy,
I make a present of Shechem,
I hand out Succoth Valley as a gift.
MSG.8:
Gilead's in my pocket,
to say nothing of Manasseh.
Ephraim's my hard hat,
Judah my hammer.
MSG.9:
Moab's a scrub bucket—
I mop the floor with Moab,
Spit on Edom,
rain fireworks all over Philistia."
MSG.10:
Who will take me to the thick of the fight?
Who'll show me the road to Edom?
MSG.11:
You aren't giving up on us, are you, God?
refusing to go out with our troops?
MSG.12:
Give us help for the hard task;
human help is worthless.
MSG.13:
In God we'll do our very best;
he'll flatten the opposition for good.

NIV.1:
Psalm 108
A song. A psalm of David.
My heart is steadfast, O God;
I will sing and make music with all my soul.
NIV.2:
Awake, harp and lyre!
I will awaken the dawn.
NIV.3:
I will praise you, O Lord, among the nations;
I will sing of you among the peoples.
NIV.4:
For great is your love, higher than the heavens;
your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
NIV.5:
Be exalted, O God, above the heavens,
and let your glory be over all the earth.
NIV.6:
Save us and help us with your right hand,
that those you love may be delivered.
NIV.7:
God has spoken from his sanctuary:
“In triumph I will parcel out Shechem
and measure off the Valley of Succoth.
NIV.8:
Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine;
Ephraim is my helmet,
Judah my scepter.
NIV.9:
Moab is my washbasin,
upon Edom I toss my sandal;
over Philistia I shout in triumph.”
NIV.10:
Who will bring me to the fortified city?
Who will lead me to Edom?
NIV.11:
Is it not you, O God, you who have rejected us
and no longer go out with our armies?
NIV.12:
Give us aid against the enemy,
for the help of man is worthless.
NIV.13:
With God we will gain the victory,
and he will trample down our enemies.

NRSVCE.1:
My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast;
I will sing and make melody.
Awake, my soul!
NRSVCE.2:
Awake, O harp and lyre!
I will awake the dawn.
NRSVCE.3:
I will give thanks to you, O LORD, among the peoples,
and I will sing praises to you among the nations.
NRSVCE.4:
For your steadfast love is higher than the heavens,
and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
NRSVCE.5:
Be exalted, O God, above the heavens,
and let your glory be over all the earth.
NRSVCE.6:
Give victory with your right hand, and answer me,
so that those whom you love may be rescued.
NRSVCE.7:
God has promised in his sanctuary:
“With exultation I will divide up Shechem,
and portion out the Vale of Succoth.
NRSVCE.8:
Gilead is mine; Manasseh is mine;
Ephraim is my helmet;
Judah is my scepter.
NRSVCE.9:
Moab is my washbasin;
on Edom I hurl my shoe;
over Philistia I shout in triumph.”
NRSVCE.10:
Who will bring me to the fortified city?
Who will lead me to Edom?
NRSVCE.11:
Have you not rejected us, O God?
You do not go out, O God, with our armies.
NRSVCE.12:
O grant us help against the foe,
for human help is worthless.
NRSVCE.13:
With God we shall do valiantly;
it is he who will tread down our foes.

NET.1: I am determined, O God! I will sing and praise you with my whole heart.
NET.2: Awake, O stringed instrument and harp! I will wake up at dawn!
NET.3: I will give you thanks before the nations, O LORD! I will sing praises to you before foreigners!
NET.4: For your loyal love extends beyond the sky, and your faithfulness reaches the clouds.
NET.5: Rise up above the sky, O God! May your splendor cover the whole earth!
NET.6: Deliver by your power and answer me, so that the ones you love may be safe.
NET.7: God has spoken in his sanctuary: “I will triumph! I will parcel out Shechem, the valley of Succoth I will measure off.
NET.8: Gilead belongs to me, as does Manasseh! Ephraim is my helmet, Judah my royal scepter.
NET.9: Moab is my wash basin. I will make Edom serve me. I will shout in triumph over Philistia.”
NET.10: Who will lead me into the fortified city? Who will bring me to Edom?
NET.11: Have you not rejected us, O God? O God, you do not go into battle with our armies.
NET.12: Give us help against the enemy, for any help men might offer is futile.
NET.13: By God’s power we will conquer; he will trample down our enemies.
